name: Powder blue
notes: Primary Rococo blue; used for walls, sky, silk upholstery, and dress fabric.

name: Blush rose
notes: Soft pink; dresses, ribbons, upholstery, boudoir walls. Associated with Boucher's palette.

name: Celadon mint
notes: Cool green accent; used in textiles, porcelain, garden-adjacent scenes.

name: Cream pearl
notes: Neutral ground; boiserie panel fields, plasterwork, lace, background walls.

name: Pale yellow
notes: Warm light accent; silk, candle-lit interior warmth, sky at dawn in ceiling frescoes.

name: Gilded gold
notes: Gold leaf and gilded bronze; frames, mirror surrounds, furniture mounts, stucco highlights. Used selectively.

name: Ivory white
notes: Pure white variant for stucco, painted boiserie, and plaster ceiling fields.

name: Dusty lavender
notes: Soft violet-grey; used in silk and textile accents, secondary drapery colors.

name: Soft sage
notes: Muted garden green; foliage in pastoral and fête galante scenes.

name: Warm terracotta light
notes: Warm skin tone base and architectural warmth in candlelit salon scenes.

name: Sky fresco blue
notes: Bright pale blue for open sky in ceiling frescoes and trompe-l'œil sky openings.
